title: The Group Project Case
situation: A group has five members. Three members worked hard. Two members did not help, but they still want the same grade.
question: Should everyone receive the same grade?

title: The Lost Wallet Case
situation: A student found a wallet with money inside. Instead of returning it, the student kept it.
question: Is keeping the wallet fair?

title: The Favorite Student Case
situation: A teacher always chooses the same student to lead activities, even when others want to try.
question: Is this fair to the class?

title: The Bullying Witness Case
situation: A student saw a classmate being bullied but stayed silent.
question: Is staying silent fair to the victim?

title: The Exclusion Case
situation: A group did not allow one student to join a game because of appearance or status.
question: Is excluding the student fair?

title: The Sharing Case
situation: One student takes most of the art materials during group work. Others do not have enough materials.
question: Is this fair to the group?

title: The Rule-Breaking Case
situation: A student breaks a school rule because they think the rule is not important.
question: Is breaking the rule fair to others?

title: The Speaking Time Case
situation: During a group discussion, one student talks all the time and does not give others a chance to speak.
question: Is this fair during group discussion?
